alerts.name: Cross Site Scripting (Reflected)
alerts.desc: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) is an attack technique that involves echoing attacker-supplied code into a user's browser instance.
alerts.solution: Validate all input and encode output. Use a context-aware output encoding library.

alerts.name: Absence of Anti-CSRF Tokens
alerts.desc: No Anti-CSRF tokens were found in a HTML submission form.
alerts.solution: Use a vetted library or framework that does not allow this weakness to occur or provides constructs that make it easier to avoid.

alerts.name: Cookie No HttpOnly Flag
alerts.desc: A cookie has been set without the HttpOnly flag, which means that the cookie can be accessed by JavaScript.
alerts.solution: Ensure that the HttpOnly flag is set for all cookies.

alerts.name: Information Disclosure - Sensitive Information in URL
alerts.desc: The request appeared to contain sensitive information leaked in the URL. This can violate PCI and most organizational compliance policies.
alerts.solution: Do not pass sensitive information in URIs.
